modules.module_title: Essential Vocabulary: Days, Months, Numbers & Relations
Basic Sentence Construction, Nouns, Verbs & Pronouns
Professional Ethics, Cultural Etiquette & Business Communication

questions.question: Urdu language kis script style me likhi jati hai?

options.A: Nastaliq Script (Right to Left / Daaye se baaye)
options.D: Urdu has no specific writing direction rules

questions.explanation: Urdu ko right-to-left format me Nastaliq calligraphic style script ke under write aur read kiya jata hai.

questions.question: Urdu conversation checks me, 'Aadaab' (آداب) gesture ka kya meaning hota hai?

options.A: Polite greeting / Respectful welcome (shishtachar aur salamti)

questions.explanation: Aadaab common secular respectful greeting gesture hai jo custom respect portray karta hai.

questions.question: Urdu vocabulary checks me, gratitude (dhanyawad) show karne ke liye kaun sa standard word use hota hai?

options.A: Shukriya (شکریہ)

questions.explanation: Shukriya is the baseline standard Urdu word for expressing thanks or gratitude.

questions.question: Urdu letters (Huroof-e-Tahajji) checks me total letters count standard kya mana jata hai?

options.A: 39 basic letters (with some regional variations up to 40+)

questions.explanation: Urdu alphabet me approx 39 basic huroof hote hain jo distinct sound characters define karte hain.

questions.question: Urdu daily greetings checks me afternoon/evening wishes me 'Shab Bakhair' (شب بخیر) ka kya meaning hota hai?

options.A: Good Night (raat ka salam/alvida)

questions.explanation: Shab Bakhair means Good Night. It is spoken when departing or retiring to sleep.

questions.question: Urdu grammar checks me, masculine gender 'Larka' (ladka) ko plural form me kya bola jata hai?

options.A: Larke (لڑکے - boys)

questions.explanation: Plural masculine nouns suffix checks shift singular ending 'a' sound to 'e' sound.

questions.question: Urdu polite conversations checks me, 'Apna parichay' (self-introduction) start sentence template?

options.A: 'Mera naam [Name] hai aur main [City] ka rehne wala hoon.'
options.B: 'Listen to me, I will say my name now.'

questions.explanation: 'Mera naam...' standard polite introduction syntax rules ko satisfy karta hai.

questions.question: Urdu numbers checks me, number 'Chaar' (چار) kis digit values ko match karta hai?

options.A: 4 (Four)

questions.explanation: Urdu counting: Ek (1), Do (2), Teen (3), Chaar (4). So Chaar is 4.

questions.question: Urdu poetry/adab terms checks me 'Ghazal' (غزل) ka literal meaning kya hota hai?

options.A: Talking to or about beloved women (mohabbat bhari guftagu)

questions.explanation: Ghazal matches poetic style lines portraying feelings, historically referencing love and devotion expressions.

questions.question: Cultural ethics checks me, guest welcoming (mehman nawazi) in Urdu culture key principles?

options.A: Serving water/tea immediately, showing high warmth, and using respectful titles (eg 'Aap', 'Tashreef rakhiye')
options.B: Ignoring guests for 30 minutes in lobby

questions.explanation: Polite indicators like 'Tashreef rakhiye' (please sit) are core hospitality standards in Urdu-speaking cultures.

questions.question: Urdu numbers checks me, number 'Teen' (تین) represents which digit?

options.A: 3 (Three)

questions.explanation: Teen represents digit 3. It is written as ۳ in Urdu numerals.

questions.question: Urdu vocabulary checks what does the word 'Ghar' (گھر) mean?

options.A: House (ghar)

questions.explanation: Ghar is a common Urdu word meaning house or home, identical to Hindi.

questions.question: Urdu vocabulary what is the meaning of word 'Kitab' (کتاب)?

options.A: Book (pustak)

questions.explanation: Kitab is the Urdu word for book, derived from Arabic.

questions.question: Urdu greetings what does 'Khuda Hafiz' (خدا حافظ) mean?

options.A: Goodbye (May God protect you)

questions.explanation: Khuda Hafiz is the standard goodbye phrase in Urdu, meaning 'May God be your protector'.

questions.question: Urdu adab why is using 'Aap' (آپ) instead of 'Tum' (تم) preferred when addressing elders?

options.A: Reflects respect and high cultural etiquette (tehzeeb)

questions.explanation: Using 'Aap' is standard respectful pronoun etiquette, displaying good upbringing and cultural polish.

options.A: 3 (Three)

questions.explanation: Teen represents digit 3. It is written as ۳ in Urdu numerals.

questions.question: Urdu grammar 'Compound sounds' (mushaddad) what is the role of the 'Shadda' (ّ) diacritic?

options.A: Doubles the pronunciation of the letter it sits on (tashdeed)

questions.explanation: Shadda (ّ) indicates that the consonant is doubled or pronounced with extra force, crucial for correct pronunciation.

questions.question: Urdu greetings what does 'Khush Amdeed' (خوش آمدید) mean?

options.A: Welcome (swagat)

questions.explanation: Khush Amdeed is the formal Urdu term for welcoming guests, equivalent to 'welcome'.

questions.question: Urdu vocabulary 'Months' what is the name for the month of fasting in the Islamic calendar?

options.A: Ramadan (رمضان)

questions.explanation: Ramadan is the holy month of fasting, widely recognized in Islamic and Urdu-speaking cultural loops.

questions.question: Urdu dialogue prompts 'Asking for help' how to say 'Can you help me?' politely?

options.A: Kya aap meri madad kar sakte hain? (کیا آپ میری مدد کر سکتے ہیں؟)

questions.explanation: Kya aap meri madad... is the standard polite formulation for requesting assistance from others.

questions.question: Urdu cultural values 'Tehzeeb' how is tea served to a guest?

options.A: Served on a tray, using the right hand, with a polite phrase like 'Tashreef laiye' or 'Lijiye'
options.B: Passed informally with left hand while watching TV
options.C: Left on table corner without saying anything

questions.explanation: Traditional tehzeeb dictates serving refreshments respectfully using the right hand and offering polite words.

questions.question: Urdu alphabet 'Aspirated sounds' (do-chashmi hay) how are they written?

options.A: Using 'جھ', 'تھ', 'پھ' (combining basic letter with do-chashmi hay 'ھ')
options.C: Written with double dots on top

questions.explanation: Aspirated consonants (hakar-yukta sounds like bh, ph, th) are formed by joining the letter with do-chashmi hay (ھ).

questions.question: Urdu polite requests 'Apologizing' how to say 'Excuse me' or 'Sorry' politely?

options.A: Maazirat chahta hoon (معذرت چاہتا ہوں - I apologize)

questions.explanation: Maazirat chahta hoon is the formal and most polite way to express apology or regret in Urdu.

questions.question: Urdu vocabulary 'Relations' what is the word for 'Sister'?

options.A: Behan (بہن - sister)

questions.explanation: Behan is Urdu for Sister, Walid is Father, Walida is Mother, and Bhai is Brother.

questions.question: Urdu dialogue prompts 'Asking for directions' how to ask 'Where is the market?'?

options.A: Bazaar kahan hai? (بازار کہاں ہے؟)

questions.explanation: 'Bazaar' is market, 'kahan hai' is where is it. Combined, they query locations politely.

questions.question: Urdu language history what is the origin meaning of the word 'Urdu'?

options.A: Lashkar / Army Camp (Turki origin word meaning camp/army)

questions.explanation: The word 'Urdu' comes from the Turkish word 'Ordu' meaning army camp, representing its origins in military camps.

questions.question: Aspirated sounds do chashmi hay ھ?

options.A: Using 'جھ', 'تھ', 'پھ' (combining basic letter with do-chashmi hay 'ھ')
options.C: Written with double dots on top

questions.explanation: Aspirated consonants (hakar-yukta sounds like bh, ph, th) are formed by joining the letter with do-chashmi hay (ھ).

questions.question: Urdu grammar 'Pronouns singular' what is third-person singular 'He' or 'She'?

options.A: Woh (وہ - he/she/it)

questions.explanation: 'Woh' is the gender-neutral third-person singular pronoun in Urdu, representing he, she, or it depending on verb conjugation.

questions.question: Urdu numbers checks 'Do-lakh' (2,00,000) how is lakh written in Urdu?

options.A: Lakh (لاکھ)

questions.explanation: Lakh represents 100,000. Hazar is 1,000, and Kror is 10,000,000.

questions.question: Urdu polite dialogues 'Congratulating' how to say congratulations on a happy event?

options.A: Mubarakbaad! (مبارکباد - congratulations)

questions.explanation: Mubarakbaad is the formal Urdu word to congratulate someone, often paired with 'bohot bohot' (many congratulations).

questions.question: Urdu travel phrases 'Asking ticket' how to ask 'Where is the ticket counter?'?

options.A: Ticket ghar kahan hai? (ٹکٹ گھر کہاں ہے؟)

questions.explanation: Ticket ghar represents ticket counter or booking window. 'Kahan hai' is where is it.

questions.question: Urdu cultural ethics 'Tehzeeb' why is interrupting someone's speech considered rude?

options.A: Violates conversational flow and respect limits (guftagu ke aadaab)

questions.explanation: Tehzeeb mandates listening fully before responding, establishing mutual respect in conversations.

questions.question: Third person singular Woh Urdu?

options.A: Woh (وہ - he/she/it)

questions.explanation: 'Woh' is the gender-neutral third-person singular pronoun in Urdu, representing he, she, or it depending on verb conjugation.

questions.question: Urdu grammar 'Verbs' what is verb infinitive marker suffix?

options.A: Naa (نا - eg Bolna, Likhna, Parhna)

questions.explanation: Verbs in their dictionary infinitive forms end with the suffix 'naa' (eg Bolna - to speak, Likhna - to write).

questions.question: Urdu vocabulary 'Colors' what is color 'Safaid' (white)?

options.A: White (safaid)

questions.explanation: Safaid is white, Kala is black, Peela is yellow, and Hara/Sabz is green.

questions.question: Urdu daily conversations 'Inquiring health' how to ask 'How is your health?' politely?

options.A: Aap ki tabiyat kaisi hai? (آپ کی طبیعت کیسی ہے؟)

questions.explanation: Aap ki tabiyat... is the specific respectful formula for querying someone's physical health or well-being.

questions.question: Urdu literature 'Couplet' what is the term for a two-line verse in poetry?

options.A: She'r (شعر - couplet)

questions.explanation: A She'r is a complete poetic couplet composed of two lines (Misras), forming the foundation of Ghazals.

questions.question: Urdu business interactions 'Polite refusal' how to decline a proposal politely?

options.A: Main iske liye maazirat chahta hoon (میں اس کے لیے معذرت چاہتا ہوں - I apologize I cannot do this)

questions.explanation: Using maazirat (apology/regret) cushions refusal, maintaining polite business relationships.

questions.question: Urdu alphabet 'Mixed characters' what is the sound of 'Noon Ghunnah' (ں)?

options.A: Nasal sound (bindu sound like in 'Maa' or 'Hum')

questions.explanation: Noon Ghunnah (ں) has no dot and is used to nasalize the vowel sound of the letter preceding it.

questions.question: Urdu polite expressions 'Asking name' how to ask a stranger's name respectfully?

options.A: Aap ka ism-e-shareef kya hai? (آپ کا اسم شریف کیا ہے؟)

questions.explanation: 'Ism-e-shareef' is the highly formal and respectful way to ask for someone's name in Urdu tehzeeb.

questions.question: Urdu vocabulary 'Office terms' what is the word for 'File' or 'Document'?

options.A: Dastawez (دستاویز - document)

questions.explanation: Dastawez means document or official paperwork, while daftar is the office building/room.

questions.question: Urdu dialogue prompts 'Requesting bill' how to ask for the bill at a restaurant?

options.A: Bill kitna hua? (بل کتنا ہوا؟)

questions.explanation: 'Bill kitna hua' queries the total payable amount politely in hospitality or retail settings.

questions.question: Urdu business culture 'Addressing colleagues' what suffix is added to names for respect?

options.A: Sahab (صاحب - eg Ahmed Sahab, Verma Sahab)

questions.explanation: Adding 'Sahab' (Mr./Sir equivalent) after a surname or first name is standard professional etiquette in Urdu culture.

questions.question: Urdu grammar 'Tenses' what is future tense auxiliary verb marker?

options.A: Ga / Gey / Gi (گا، گے، گی - eg Karega, Karengey, Karegi)

questions.explanation: Future tense verb endings rely on 'ga/gey/gi' auxiliary suffixes (eg 'Main kal daftar jaoonga' - I will go to office tomorrow).

questions.question: Urdu vocabulary 'Months' what is name of Islamic month of pilgrimage?

options.A: Zil-Hajj (ذوالحجہ)

questions.explanation: Zil-Hajj is the month of Hajj (pilgrimage) and Eid-ul-Adha, a key calendar month in Urdu-speaking communities.

questions.question: Urdu polite speech 'Pardoning' what is response to 'Ghalti ho gayi' (a mistake was made)?

options.A: Koi baat nahi, ehsaan hai (کوئی بات نہیں - It is fine / No problem)

questions.explanation: 'Koi baat nahi' is the standard polite formulation to accept an apology or pardon minor oversights.

questions.question: Urdu grammar 'Adjectives' what is the adjective in 'Khoonkhar Sher' (ferocious lion)?

options.A: Khoonkhar (ferocious/dangerous)

questions.explanation: Khoonkhar (ferocious) is the adjective describing the quality of the noun Sher (lion).

questions.question: Urdu business hospitality 'Gift giving' what is standard etiquette when offered a gift?

options.A: Accept politely with both hands, expressing gratitude using Shukriya or JazakAllah
options.C: Open and inspect gift value immediately on spot

questions.explanation: Accepting gifts with both hands and showing sincere gratitude is standard respect behavior in Asian cultures.
